data.title: Salesforce and Google Cloud expand AI partnership for autonomous workflows
data.description:
• Salesforce (CRM) and Google Cloud are deepening integration to enable AI agents to execute end-to-end workflows across both platforms.
  • The partnership aims to solve fragmented data and disconnected systems while reducing data movement and context switching.
  • AI agents will operate within tools like Slack and Google Workspace.
  • Platforms including Agentforce and Gemini Enterprise will provide intelligence and contextual decision-making.
  • The collaboration supports a shift from manual processes to more autonomous, AI-driven enterprise operations.
  • Integrations will span multiple layers of enterprise systems, including context, workflow execution, and user engagement.
  • CRM shares up 1% premarket.

data.title: McKesson declares $0.82 dividend
data.description:
• McKesson (MCK) declares $0.82/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.39%
  • Payable July 1; for shareholders of record June 1; ex-div June 1.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.82 for four consecutive quarters.
  • See MCK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: RenX sees Q1 revenue above $3.5M, signaling sequential growth
data.description:
RenX Enterprises (RENX) Wednesday said it expects to report revenue in excess of $3.5M for the first quarter of 2026, reflecting sequential growth over the fourth quarter of 2025.

The expected growth reflects anticipated stronger material sales at the company’s Myakka City organics processing facility and an expected meaningful step-up in logistics revenue across the company’s transportation operations.

First-quarter material sales at Myakka City are expected to include processed mulch, compost, and engineered soil products, reflecting the sequential growth from the company's fourth quarter of 2025. This growth is supported by strong customer demand for the company’s highly processed mulch and engineered soil product lines. 

The company’s logistics segment, operated through its Zimmer Equipment operations, is also expected to report higher revenue for the first quarter of 2026 relative to the fourth quarter of 2025, supported by strong route utilization and continued hauling activity across its organic waste transportation network.

data.title: Farmers National Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.08, revenue of $56.26M beats by $1.46M
data.description:
• Farmers National press release (FMNB): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $56.26M (+25.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.46M.

data.title: Axe Compute skyrockets 166% over $260M contract for Nvidia B300 GPUs
data.description:
• Axe Compute (AGPU) stock price skyrocketed above 166% to $13.61 a share as it signed a $260M, three-year enterprise contract to deploy a large-scale AI infrastructure cluster powered by Nvidia (NVDA) B300 GPUs.
  • The agreement covers the delivery of 2,304 NVIDIA B300 GPUs along with AI-focused high-speed storage, all hosted in a single U.S.-based Tier 3 data center.
  • The 36-month contract includes renewal options and is structured with deposits, prepayments, and monthly advance payments under a take-or-pay model, providing Axe Compute with long-term revenue visibility.
  • “This agreement is a signal. Enterprise AI customers are no longer willing to adapt their infrastructure roadmaps to the capacity constraints of legacy hyperscalers. A 2,304-GPU B300 deployment, contracted, dedicated, U.S.-based, and priced to compete, is what purpose-built AI infrastructure looks like. We intend to replicate this commercial structure at scale.” said CEO, Christopher Miglino.

data.title: ABB shares hit record after forecast hike fueled by data center boom
data.description:
ABB (ABBNY) shares climbed to an all-time high on Wednesday after the Swiss industrial technology group raised its full-year sales and profitability outlook, helped by strong demand tied to data centers and power infrastructure.

The stock rose nearly 6% in early trading as investors responded to stronger-than-expected first-quarter results and an improved outlook for 2026.

ABB (ABBNY) said rapid expansion in data centers, along with momentum across its electrification division, has helped counter uncertainty linked to the war in Iran and broader geopolitical risks.

The company is also benefiting from increased spending by utilities upgrading electrical grids, as well as investment in electrified transport systems and automation projects at ports and cruise terminals.

ABB (ABBNY) now expects 2026 comparable revenue growth in the high single-digit to low double-digit percentage range, up from prior guidance of 6% to 9%.

It also said its operational earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortization margin should improve this year, compared with earlier guidance that called for only a modest increase.

Strong outlook contrasts with economic caution

ABB’s (ABBNY) upbeat tone stands out against a more cautious global economic backdrop. The International Monetary Fund recently lowered its global growth forecast, citing rising energy costs associated with the Iran conflict.

Chief Executive Morten Wierod said the company’s stronger guidance reflects healthy demand trends and continued resilience among customers.

Wierod added that demand for automation and electrification products should strengthen further as energy consumption rises and companies pursue productivity improvements. He also said demand from AI-linked data center projects remains on a rapid growth trajectory.

Orders surge past expectations

ABB (ABBNY) reported first-quarter sales of $8.73 billion, up 18% from a year earlier and above analyst expectations of $8.43 billion.

Operational EBITA rose to $2.05 billion, topping forecasts of $1.96 billion, supported by stronger margins.

Orders increased 32% to $11.29 billion, setting a quarterly record and providing further support for the company’s upgraded outlook.

data.title: Robinhood Ventures fund invests $75M in OpenAI
data.description:
Robinhood Ventures Fund I (RVI) stock surged 7.5% in Wednesday premarket trading after it disclosed that it purchased ~$75M of OpenAI's  (OPENAI) common stock.

The fund went public through an initial public offering in March, providing a channel for retail investors to take stakes in private companies, an investment class once the domain of institutional investors and ultra-wealthy individuals.

"As one of RVI’s largest investments to date, this underscores our core mission to provide everyday investors with access to what we believe are transformative companies shaping the future," said Sarah Pinto, president of Robinhood Ventures Fund I.

The investment in OpenAI (OPENAI) reflects an apparent reconciliation between Robinhood (HOOD) and the AI giant. In July 2025, Robinhood started offering tokenized shares of OpenAI and SpaceX to its customers in Europe.

At the time, OpenAI warned investors that it had no part in the product. "We did not partner with Robinhood, were not involved in this, and do not endorse it," the company said on X.

data.title: Masco jumps after earnings beat, steady outlook
data.description:
Masco (MAS) surged 8.9% in premarket trading Wednesday after the maker of Behr paint, Delta faucets and other home improvement products posted results that topped Wall Street expectations and maintained its full-year outlook.

The company reported first-quarter net sales of $1.92 billion, up 6% from a year earlier and above the Wall Street consensus estimate of $1.83 billion. Adjusted earnings rose 20% to $1.04 a share, beating analyst expectations of $0.88.

Net income rose to $213 million, or $1.05 a share, from $186 million, or $0.87 a share, a year earlier.

Before Wednesday, Masco (MAS) shares had risen 11% over the prior 12 months as investors weighed resilient repair-and-remodel demand against a still-choppy housing market.

CEO cites strong execution

“We delivered strong first quarter operating results in an extremely dynamic environment,” Chief Executive Jon Nudi said in the earnings release.

He said Masco (MAS) posted sales growth of 6%, adjusted operating profit growth of 13% and adjusted earnings-per-share growth of 20% during the quarter.

Plumbing leads growth

Masco (MAS) said plumbing products revenue increased 9% to $1.36 billion, helped by gains across faucets, bath and shower fixtures. Decorative architectural products, which includes Behr paint, posted sales roughly in line with the prior year at $554 million.

Companywide operating profit increased 10% to $316 million, while adjusted operating profit rose 13% to $324 million. Adjusted operating margin expanded to 16.9% from 16.0% a year earlier.

Full-year guidance maintained

Masco (MAS) maintained 2026 earnings guidance of $3.91 to $4.11 a share, and adjusted earnings guidance of $4.10 to $4.30 a share. The adjusted range brackets Wall Street’s consensus estimate of $4.17.

Management said it was prudent to hold the forecast steady given what it called a dynamic macroeconomic and geopolitical backdrop.

Cash returns continue

Masco (MAS) repurchased 3.1 million shares for $202 million during the quarter and returned a total of $267 million to shareholders through dividends and buybacks. Liquidity at quarter-end stood at $1.26 billion.

data.title: Classover, ICreate to explore AI robotics education partnership in North America
data.description:
• Classover Holdings (KIDZ) on Wednesday said it entered a strategic collaboration with ICreate Education Technology for the development of AI and robotics learning programs in North America.
  • Shares of Classover were up 22% in premarket trading.
  • The non-binding arrangement will focus on potential classroom, robotics lab, after-school, and summer camp applications where students can interact with robotics and AI systems, according to Classover.
  • Classover would contribute its North American education network, operational infrastructure, and institutional partnerships, while ICreate would provide robotics systems and technical expertise.
  • The companies said the collaboration could include pilot programs, demonstration projects, and joint participation in industry events.
  • Classover said the agreement does not include binding commercial terms such as pricing, exclusivity, or distribution arrangements.

data.title: Lifezone Metals raises $25M via share sale at $4.40
data.description:
• Lifezone Metals (LZM) agreed to sell 5.7M shares to institutional investors at $4.40 per share, raising about $25M, the company said on Wednesday.
  • The net proceeds of this offering will be used for exploration activities in Burundi and Tanzania, the PGM Recycling Project, conducting hydromet research and development at Simulus Laboratory, and other general corporate and working capital purposes, including financing costs.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about April 23. Roth Capital Partners served as the sole placement agent for the transaction.
